summary: This paper primarily explores quantum theory, consciousness, and cyclical time. It contains very little direct information about Immanuel Kant, only briefly referencing his 'Copernican Revolution' in philosophy as an analogy for a paradigm shift where the observer (subject) becomes central to understanding the observed universe (object).

principles.statement: Philosophical priority must shift from the object to the subject.
principles.rationale: Just as Copernicus shifted the center of the solar system to the sun, philosophy requires a paradigm shift where the focus moves from the objective universe to the subjective observer's role in experiencing it.
principles.source_quote: The philosopher Immanuel Kant discussed a philosophical version of the Copernican Revolution in which the priority shifted from the object to the subject...

mental_models.name: Philosophical Copernican Revolution
mental_models.description: A paradigm shift in perspective that re-centers understanding around the subject (the observer) rather than the object (the observed), analogous to the astronomical shift from an Earth-centric to a Sun-centric model.
mental_models.example: Used as an analogy to explain the shift in modern physics where scientific investigation is viewed as an interaction between the subject and the object, rather than assuming the subject is merely a passive part of the objective universe.
